title: SQL Generation Excluding No-Migration Fields Investigation
description:
After setting a batch of fields to "No Migration" status, the generated SQL output appeared to hide some fields. Investigation revealed this was intentional behavior — fields marked "No Migration" are excluded from the SQL output since they are not part of the migration scope.

### Observed Behavior
- Fields set to "No Migration" are excluded from generated SQL
- This appeared unexpected initially but was confirmed as the correct design
- Fields that are being migrated continue to appear in the SQL output
acceptance_criteria: Fields with No Migration status are excluded from SQL generation
Fields with active migration statuses are included in SQL output
middleware_changes: SQL generation logic confirmed to filter out fields with No Migration status.
implementation_notes: This was an investigation ticket. The behavior was intentional — No Migration fields are excluded from SQL output by design since they will not be migrated.
comment_summary: Developer explained that No Migration fields were intentionally excluded from the SQL output, as this condition was part of the original implementation.
